meta.title: Nigeria Biology / Anatomy Foundation (UTME-aligned)
meta.source: Structured from public WAEC/JAMB biology topic outlines + NERDC science progression (not scraped; human-curated pack). Replace/extend via new pack versions.

concepts.title: Cells — the basic unit of life
misconceptions: plants get food only from soil
localHooks: rooms in a block of flats = cells
concepts.microLesson: Everything in your body is built from cells — tiny living units, like rooms in a big house (your body). Tissues are groups of similar cells working together. Anatomy path: cell → tissue → organ → system.

concepts.title: Cell structure (simple)
objectives: Contrast plant vs animal cell basics
concepts.microLesson: Animal cell: membrane (gate), cytoplasm (work space), nucleus (DNA control). Plant cells add wall + chloroplasts. For anatomy, master animal cell first.

concepts.title: Four main tissue types
concepts.microLesson: Epithelial covers, connective supports, muscle moves, nervous signals. Organs are multi-tissue structures.

concepts.title: Organs and systems
concepts.microLesson: Organs (heart, liver, brain) combine tissues. Systems are organ teams. Anatomy = structure; physiology = function.

concepts.title: Skeletal system overview
concepts.microLesson: Skeleton supports, protects, enables movement with muscles. Axial vs appendicular. Bone is living tissue.

concepts.title: Anatomical direction words
concepts.microLesson: Anterior/posterior, superior/inferior, medial/lateral, proximal/distal — shared map language for anatomy.

concepts.title: Digestive system introduction
concepts.microLesson: Food path: mouth → oesophagus → stomach → small intestine → large intestine. Digestion breaks food; absorption takes nutrients into blood.

concepts.title: Circulatory system introduction
concepts.microLesson: Heart pumps blood through vessels. Arteries away, veins toward heart, capillaries exchange. Blood carries oxygen, food, waste.
